title: Credit Edge: Amundi Flags Risk of AI Crowding Out (Podcast)
summary: Further acceleration in AI debt issuance has the potential to steer demand away from government bonds, according to Amundi, Europe’s largest asset manager. “At some point it could have a crowding effect, but we are not there yet,” Gregoire Pesques, the firm’s chief investment officer for global fixed income, tells Bloomberg News’ James Crombie and Bloomberg Intelligence’s Tolu Alamutu in the latest Credit Edge podcast. “It’s a risk that we monitor,” says Pesques, whose company has $2.7 trillion

summary: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.

title: South Korea’s ‘Ant’ Army Is Driving an AI Market Frenzy
summary: Some 14 million retail investors are piling into shares of two big tech giants, but worries are growing about what happens if chip demand dries up.

title: Apple Prices Climb and the AI Trade Roars Back | Open Interest 6/25/2026
summary:
Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest."The AI trade is roaring back after Micron’s blowout forecast. JPMorgan shakes up succession plans for Jamie Dimon. Ford CEO Jim Farley weighs in on AI versus experience, and Apple raises prices as the chip shortage hits. Plus, Bob Sloan on market positioning risks and Adam Hetts on why earnings still matter.  Correct: Ford is aiming to cut $1 billion in costs this year. 
The caption has been updated to reflect the the correct savings figure on controllable costs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
summary: ホルムズ海峡で船舶に攻撃、米PCE価格と消費支出、アップル値上げ、JPモルガンCEO後任争いで新局面、自動車部品マレリの資産取得協議

title: Trump-Backed Golf Resort Advances as Vietnam Seizes Farmland
summary: Eviction notices have been sent to farmers in northern Vietnam to make way for a $1.5 billion Trump Organization-branded luxury golf resort project that’s already behind schedule.

title: UK’s Crown Estate Profits Fall as Offshore Wind Fees Decline
summary: Profits at the Crown Estate, the property company that generates income for King Charles III, fell last year as a temporary boost from the licensing of new offshore wind farms began to fade.

title: Stocks Whipsaw as Micron Surges While Apple Sinks: Markets Wrap
summary: A renewed wave of tech volatility gripped Wall Street, with a selloff in megacaps tempering optimism in other corners of the stock market that were buoyed by signs the economy is in good shape.

title: Down Jones Up, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Down | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Erase Gains as Apple Price Hikes Offset Chips Strength
summary: US stock indexes ended Thursday nearly where they started after a choppy trading session that saw Apple Inc.’s move to raise prices on its products unwinding some of the enthusiasm over strong chipmaker forecasts.

title: Venezuela Searches for Survivors After Quakes
summary: Twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, causing widespread destruction in the coastal city of Catia La Mar and the coastal state of La Guaira. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hertz Eyes Record Weekly Drop on Funding Plan, Profit Guide
summary: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock continued for a second day.

title: US Airline Stocks Erase Pandemic Losses as Oil Prices Ease
summary: A popular gauge of the US airline industry has finally recovered from pandemic-era losses after six years, as progress on a peace deal between US and Iran pushed oil prices lower and eased the pressure on carriers’ profitability.

title: Apple Shares Sink After Price Hikes Hit iPads and Macs
summary: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.

title: Trump Seeks $11.1 Billion in New Aid for Struggling Farmers
summary: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited for dinner at the White House later Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.

title: Confident In Growth Next Three Years: Casey CEO
summary: Casey CEO Darren Rebelez discusses company growth, business in Texas, competition, food expansion. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Traders Trim Fed Rate-Hike Bets After Benign Inflation Data
summary: Bond traders priced in slightly lower expectations for a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike in the months ahead after the central bank’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated.

title: US Soccer Looks To Capitalize on The World Cup Attention
summary: JT Batson, US Soccer CEO andRandall Williams, Bloomberg News Senior Reporter AND Co-Host of the ‘Business of Sports’ Podcast join Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss World Cup engagement and partnerships, fan and viewership metrics, popularity and the tournament's overall commercial expansion. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blackrock Survey Says Americans Looking Beyond Savings for Retirement
summary:
Nick Nefouse, Global Head of Retirement Solutions and Head of LifePath at BlackRock joined Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their latest retirement survey, and retirement readiness. Nick said people that are approaching retirement need something more than stock and bond portfolios. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Moderna's Flu Vaccine Moves Closer to FDA Approval
summary: The FDA advisory committee recommended Moderna's flu vaccine for approval. Moderna CEO Stéphane Bancel tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld he is cautiously optimistic that the agency will follow the guidance. A final decision by the FDA is expected by August 5 which is just in time for the next flu season. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
summary: A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint.

title: Oil Gains as Ship Attack Raises Concern About Hormuz Reopening
summary: Oil rose as traders closely monitored shipments through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel was struck by a projectile in the key shipping corridor off the Omani coast, renewing concerns about the safety for passing cargoes amid the US and Iran’s peace negotiations.

title: Too Early to Worry About Concentration Risk: Doyle
summary:
Stephanie Doyle, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, Viktor Hjort, global head of credit strategy & desks analysts at BNP Paribas, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Endless Shrimp Deal Was Scheme to Squeeze Red Lobster, Suit Says
summary: Red Lobster’s $20 all-you-can-eat shrimp deal wasn’t just a badly designed promotion that inadvertently helped tip the iconic American restaurant chain into bankruptcy.

title: SpaceX Sale Shows Funding Across Equity, Credit: Hjort
summary: Viktor Hjort, global head of credit strategy & desks analysts at BNP Paribas, and Stephanie Doyle, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ford CEO Says Human Workers Are Making a Difference
summary:
Ford Motor Co. Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley talks about how human workers are making all the difference on the factory floors. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." 

Correction: Ford is aiming to cut $1 billion in costs this year. 
The caption has been updated to reflect the correct savings figure on controllable costs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fans Sit at Center of World Cup | Sports City
summary: Bloomberg's Randall Williams looks at how ticket prices keep many fans out of the World Cup, but they're still spending millions of dollars on the tournament.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Memory is a Structural Story for AI: Lovell
summary: Nadia Lovell, Head of Global Equity Strategy at UBS Global Wealth, says memory is a stickier, structural story for AI and has longer legs to go within the trade. She speaks to Bloomberg's Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ares Private Credit Fund Caps Redemptions After 14% Seek to Exit
summary:
Bloomberg's Nabila Ahmed and James Crombie joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Ares Management curbed withdrawals from one of its private credit funds for the second consecutive quarter after redemption requests rose to 14.4%, the latest sign that the $1.8 trillion industry remains under pressure.
 (Source: Bloomberg)
